title: salix × pseudoglauca
intro: Salix × pseudoglauca, also known as the false willow, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae) and is a cross between Salix purpurea and Salix caprea. The salix × pseudoglauca plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Salix × pseudoglauca can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
